Entry Thesis: GAP earnings calendar put spread - theta experiment

Mistakes: No stop mechanism. Last contract closed Friday at significantly worse prices

Entry Thesis: Theta decay experiment — compare decay across two expiry dates. Both strikes 13-15% OTM at entry (AVAV at $278)
Market Context: Crisis Volatility — Iran conflict broke over weekend, hammered markets
Risk Management Plan: Credit received: $4,280. 100% stop at $8,560 debit ($21.40/spread). No layered alerts set (pre-enhancement). Stop fired and saved $8-10k.
Exit Reasoning: Stop fired within 90 minutes as AVAV dropped sharply on Iran news. AVAV subsequently fell to $204 (26% decline). Stop saved estimated $8,000-$10,000 in further losses
Lessons Learned: THE STOP SAVED $8,000-$10,000. Market orders gave away ~30% of premium at entry. Limit orders now non-negotiable. Earnings check missed (AVAV earnings Mar 10). Experimental trades must be sized at 50% or less of standard positions
Mistakes: Market order execution (filled $5.20-$5.50 vs ~$7 mark). No earnings check (AVAV earnings 8 days away). No layered alerts. Position 4.2x standard $1,000 CSP target
Tags: Diagonal, AVAV, Experimental, Stop-Loss, Crisis, Loss

Entry Thesis: Sportradar — less Iran-sensitive. Defensive sector selection in crisis conditions

Risk Management Plan: Premium at risk: $1,530. 50% profit target ($765). 50% loss alert ($765). 100% stop ($1,530).
Exit Reasoning: Hit near 50% profit limit. 58.8% premium capture in 2 days. Best CSP discipline of the week
Lessons Learned: Clean execution, defensive stock selection pays off in crisis. 50% profit limit working as designed

Risk Management Plan: Premium at risk: $2,040. 50% profit target ($1,020). 50% loss alert ($1,020). 100% stop ($2,040).
Exit Reasoning: 39 contracts closed same day at breakeven ($0.51). Last contract held 3 days, closed at $0.95. Commission drag ($52) turned flat trade into loss
Lessons Learned: If exiting at breakeven within hours, setup quality was insufficient. Commission drag on large contract counts is significant
Mistakes: Setup quality insufficient — exited at breakeven same day

Risk Management Plan: Premium at risk: $2,920. 50% profit target ($1,460). 50% loss alert ($1,460). 100% stop ($2,920).
Exit Reasoning: Sold at $2.92, closed at $4.00 (37% adverse move). Managed exit — used 50% downside alert and decided to close. Better risk management than NVCR (-112%)
Lessons Learned: Managed exit at -37% instead of potential -100%+. Loss contained, capital preserved. 50% alert framework working
Mistakes: Could have been flagged earlier — stock moving against

Risk Management Plan: Premium at risk: $1,470. 50% profit target ($735). 50% loss alert ($735). 100% stop ($1,470). Commission drag: 3.7%.
Exit Reasoning: Stock barely moved ($0.35 to $0.36) but $54.60 in round-trip commissions turned scratch into loss. Commission was 3.7% of premium collected
Lessons Learned: Commission filter needed: reject trades where commission > 2% of premium. 42 contracts on cheap underlying = excessive commission drag
Mistakes: Too many contracts on low-premium underlying. Commission exceeded 2% of premium
